title: MDM Policy Forced a Mid-ESP Reboot (Second Sign-In)
description: Detects the 'unexpected reboot + second sign-in screen' pattern: one or more device-assigned MDM policies matched the OS reboot-required catalog during ESP DeviceSetup, and Windows coalesced them into a restart at the end of DeviceSetup — forcing the user through a second sign-in before AccountSetup. Attributes the reboot to the specific CSP policy URIs so the profiles can be reassigned.
author: Autopilot Monitor
explanation:
During ESP DeviceSetup, a device-assigned MDM policy matched the OS reboot-required catalog and Windows scheduled a **coalesced reboot** at the end of DeviceSetup — the user saw an unexpected restart and a **second sign-in screen** before AccountSetup continued.

First reboot-forcing policy URI: `{{rebootUri}}`

When several policies force a reboot they are coalesced into one restart; **each** reboot-forcing policy appears as its own `mdm_policy_reboot_required` event on the session timeline — check the timeline for the complete URI list.

This is expected OS behavior, not a failure — but it degrades the enrollment experience, confuses users, and is invisible in the Intune console.

remediation.title: Reassign the reboot-forcing policies to user groups
steps: Open each `mdm_policy_reboot_required` timeline event and note the CSP URI (e.g. `./Device/Vendor/MSFT/...`)
Map each URI to its Intune profile (Settings Catalog / configuration profile) — the URI path names the CSP area
Reassign those profiles from device groups to user groups so they apply during/after AccountSetup instead of DeviceSetup
Alternatively, exclude the Autopilot device group from the profile and deliver the setting post-enrollment

remediation.title: Or accept the reboot and set expectations
steps: If the policy must be device-assigned (e.g. a security baseline), keep it and document the expected mid-ESP restart
Communicate to users that a restart and second sign-in during provisioning is expected for these deployments

relatedDocs.title: Patch My PC: Avoid the second sign-in screen after Autopilot
